name: Westside Law Firm
description_short: Chicago-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, founded by Brian Zeft in 2015 to provide personalized debt relief and creditor harassment solutions.
description_long:
Westside Law Firm, LLC was founded in 2015 by Brian Zeft, a bankruptcy attorney with extensive experience in consumer insolvency law. Prior to opening his own practice, Zeft served as managing attorney at one of Chicago's largest bankruptcy firms, overseeing all Illinois office locations and handling thousands of bankruptcy cases. He holds a J.D. from John Marshall Law School (cum laude) and a B.S. in Business Administration from Rider University, and previously worked for a Wall Street investment bank.

The firm specializes exclusively in consumer bankruptcy law, focusing on Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings. Beyond traditional bankruptcy services, Westside Law Firm offers solutions for related financial crises including foreclosure prevention, car repossession defense, wage garnishment relief, and resolution of outstanding parking tickets. They market their approach as providing urgent intervention for clients facing multiple simultaneous financial threats.

What distinguishes this firm is Zeft's appellate litigation track record. He has argued twice before the United States Court of Appeals for the 7th Circuit, and in one landmark case (Thompson vs. General Motor Acceptance Corp., 566 F.3d 699), successfully overturned a decade of case law in the Northern District of Illinois regarding auto lender obligations to return repossessed vehicles after bankruptcy filing. The firm emphasizes personalized client attention and operates from a convenient Near West Side Chicago location with free street parking and direct CTA access.

The firm appears legitimate and competent but serves a limited geographic market (Chicago area) and is a solo/small practice without disclosed team information. While Zeft's credentials are substantial, his appellate experience may not directly translate to better outcomes for routine Chapter 7/13 cases, which typically follow established procedural paths. Clients should confirm handling of their specific case type before engagement.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Founder has argued twice before the U.S. Court of Appeals for the 7th Circuit with one major appellate victory affecting auto repossession law
Zeft previously managed all Illinois offices of a large bankruptcy firm and personally handled thousands of cases
Offers comprehensive solutions beyond bankruptcy filing, including foreclosure prevention, repossession defense, wage garnishment, and parking ticket resolution
Located in accessible Chicago neighborhood with free street parking and multiple public transit options
Founded to provide more personalized attention than large bankruptcy mills
Graduated cum laude from law school and sat on the John Marshall Law Review
cons: Solo or very small practice with no disclosed team, limiting availability and capacity
Serves only Chicago/Illinois area despite national consumer base needing bankruptcy services
No pricing information disclosed on website; free consultation required to determine costs
No client testimonials, case results, or outcomes data provided
Appellate experience may not reduce timeline or improve outcomes for straightforward Chapter 7/13 cases
best_for: Chicago-area residents facing multiple simultaneous financial crises (repossession, foreclosure, garnishment, debt)
Debtors seeking personalized attention from an experienced bankruptcy attorney rather than high-volume firm representation
Individuals with complex bankruptcy issues that may benefit from appellate-level legal expertise
Debtors needing help with secondary issues like parking ticket resolution alongside bankruptcy filing
diagnosis: Westside Law Firm is best for Chicago-area consumers facing overlapping financial crises (bankruptcy + repossession/foreclosure/garnishment) who value hands-on attorney interaction over high-volume processing. The main caveat is that this is a geographically limited, small practice with no disclosed team depth, and appellate credentials do not necessarily improve outcomes for routine bankruptcy cases that follow standard procedures.
services: Financial counseling and fresh start planning
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
